# Transcrição: 10- ACTIVITY- Beginning Your Personal Story

- **Fonte:** 10- ACTIVITY- Beginning Your Personal Story
- **Duração:** 02:00
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:00]

It's time to craft the beginning of your personal story. Before you do an exercise I want you to begin doing whenever you're watching those movies, whenever you're reading those books. Pay attention to the two words I gave you, location and action. When a movie begins, look at the location and see if it began with action. And note what the action is. It doesn't need to be amazing, exciting, thrilling action. I have stories that begin with, I'm walking across a parking lot. I'm looking at a pile of leaves. I pass my license across a counter. I sit in a chair. All of those are actions that signal to the audience, the story has begun. The goal of action is not to excite the audience with fantastic action, it's to signal to the audience that our story has begun. If you hear someone telling a story and at one point in the story you hear someone say, one day you should be thinking that is where your story should have begun. One day something happened and everything that came before it was exposition that should have been infused into the story later on. So pay attention to location and action in the movies you're watching. Now for your own story, same thing. You're going to choose a location and an action. You're going to choose a location and an action, essentially a scene that is as far along into the story as humanly possible. Push it. Push the beginning of your story as close to the ending as possible and take anything that you think we need to know, anything you need to teach us and push it all aside. We will put it into the story later on. Your beginning needs to be simple. It needs to be close to the end and it needs to engage the audience's mind with location turning on that movie reel, playing that movie in their mind and action which signals the story has begun. Figure out where you're going to start that personal story now.
